title: God’s Word – Our Daily Bread from Heaven
summary: “Give us day by day our daily bread.” (Luke 11:3). “Then said the LORD unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.” (Exodus 16:4). When the Lord Jesus told us to pray, “Give us day by day our daily bread”, the Jews who heard him no doubt thought about the miracle of the manna in the wilderness. The Lord Jesus was not just talking about earthly food, but the Word of God which is more important than our physical sustenance. One reason the prophet Job is mentioned as one of three righteous men on the earth is because he esteemed the Word of God more than physical nourishment. “Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food” (Job 23:12). Let us meditate on the importance of the Word of God to motivate us to be like Job. Humans look below to the earth for food, but God thought the children of Israel to look heavenward every day for their physical food. “And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another, It is manna: for they wist not what it was. And Moses said unto them, This is the bread which the LORD hath given you to eat.” (Exodus 16:15) “For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world. Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread. And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.” (John 6:33-35) Let us say with those Jews, “Lord, evermore give us this bread”.
